title: rosa coriifolia var. subbiserrata
intro: Rosa coriifolia var. subbiserrata is a type of wild rose plant, also known as the "Sub-biserrate Apple Rose," which belongs to the family Rosaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues, such as nausea and diarrhea, due to its anthelmintic and astringent properties. The health benefits of Rosa coriifolia var. subbiserrata include its ability to soothe and calm the digestive system, reduce inflammation, and even act as an antioxidant to protect against cell damage. The herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures for internal use, or applied topically as a cream or salve for skin issues.
